families.name: Payment delinquency history
families.note: Three severity tiers of the same behavior. Never contrast tiers against each other.

features.definition: Number of times borrower has been 30-59 days past due but no worse in the last 2 years.
features.high_value_means: More frequent mild payment delays, a moderate risk signal.

features.definition: Number of times borrower has been 60-89 days past due but no worse in the last 2 years.
features.high_value_means: More frequent moderate payment delays, a moderate to high risk signal.

features.definition: Number of times borrower has been 90 days or more past due.
features.high_value_means: A history of serious payment failure, one of the strongest signals of higher default risk.

families.name: Income reliability
families.note: These two partly reflect the same fact: income information is unreliable for this applicant. Present as one theme when both appear.

features.definition: The applicant's monthly debt payments and living costs divided by monthly gross income. A value of 0.5 means half of income is already committed.
features.high_value_means: A high ratio indicates that a large portion of the applicant's income is already committed to debt payments, suggesting potential financial strain and higher risk of default.

features.definition: Flag created during preprocessing: 1 if the applicant did not disclose income (the missing value was filled with the median income for modeling).
features.high_value_means: Income information is unreliable for this applicant. Extreme DebtRatio values frequently coincide with missing income, so the two should be read as one theme rather than independent evidence.

families.name: Credit portfolio size
families.note: Real estate lines are a subset of total open lines.

features.definition: Number of Open loans (installment like car loan or mortgage) and Lines of credit (e.g. credit cards)
features.high_value_means: A higher number of open credit lines and loans may indicate that the borrower has more financial obligations, which could increase the risk of default if not managed properly.

features.definition: Number of mortgage and real estate loans including home equity lines of credit
features.high_value_means: A higher number of real estate loans may indicate a larger financial commitment and increased risk of default.

features.definition: How much of their available credit card and personal credit limits the applicant is currently using. A value of 1.0 means fully maxed out.
features.high_value_means: A higher utilization rate indicates that the borrower is using a larger portion of their available credit, which may suggest financial stress and a higher risk of default.
Higher age is associated with lower default risk in this data, consistent with longer credit histories and more stable finances.
A higher monthly income may indicate a greater ability to meet financial obligations, potentially reducing default risk.

features.definition: Number of dependents in family excluding themselves (spouse, children etc.)
features.high_value_means: A higher number of dependents may indicate a greater financial burden and potentially increase the risk of default.
